extraction.one_line_summary: VALVERDE Alejandro won after THOMAS Geraint's attack failed 2km out, positioning perfectly in the uphill sprint at La Molina.
tactical_patterns: Sky's THOMAS attacked with 2km remaining but immediately looked around and soft-pedaled, surrendering momentum to late punchers.
Movistar sat passively despite THOMAS's attack, preserving VALVERDE's uphill sprint punch for final 200m rather than chasing.
CONTADOR's team (Trek) put PANTANO on front at 9km when catching breakaway, exploiting natural regrouping moment for acceleration.
MARTIN Dan jumped too early in final sprint, allowing VALVERDE to mark him perfectly and use superior sustained power.

rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: neutral: attacked strongly 2km out but lacked conviction to sustain, suggesting either tactical uncertainty or insufficient form for sustained acceleration
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: demonstrated superior tactical discipline by refusing to chase THOMAS, then perfect wheel choice on MARTIN's premature jump
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: negative: unable to respond when THOMAS and KENYATTA accelerated, confirming early-season form deficit as expected
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: negative: initiated sprint far too early several hundred meters out, revealing poor uphill sprint judgment despite good late-race positioning
rider_signals.VAN GARDEREN Tejay: negative: lost 22 seconds on relatively modest summit finish, concerning weakness for overall BMC leadership aspirations
